mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage derives perf_score per seed; StdPowerMutationalStage allocates more havoc iterations to faster/smaller/stable seeds, letting the structured DTD corpus accumulate enough mutations to traverse the deep parse path.
one_line: calibrated energy concentrates havoc on fast/small seeds to reach deep parse path

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score grants compact valid-XML seeds more havoc mutations per visit; flat naive energy spreads mutations uniformly, never repairing structural tokens needed to complete the entity-ref path.
one_line: calibrated power schedule amplifies mutation effort on compact valid-XML seeds

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage gives faster/smaller/stable seeds more havoc iterations; extra mutation budget on small seeds raises the probability of landing a '-', '.', or 0xB7 byte in the name position that flips the gate true.
one_line: calibrated energy raises probability of mutating a name byte to the required character class

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score grants the structured 110-122 byte seeds with the concrete 'Gte'/'TE'-token prefix more havoc energy per visit, preserving and extending the structured header; naive's flat budget only ever produces short degenerate seeds.
one_line: calibrated energy preserves and extends the structured header seed that sets the TE flag

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score (speed/size/stability, no rarity) reallocates havoc budget toward compact stable font seeds nearest the GPOS attachment structure, reaching the flag-check true side faster than naive's uniform scheduling.
one_line: calibrated energy focuses mutations on small stable font seeds approaching the GPOS flag

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage concentrates havoc mutations on small, fast, valid-font seeds; the extra per-seed mutation budget refines compact font headers into valid shaping inputs that set the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph flag, which naive's flat budget cannot achieve.
one_line: calibrated power budget refines compact font seeds into valid inputs setting UNSAFE_TO_BREAK

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score awards small/fast/stable table-bearing seeds more havoc iterations; the higher per-seed energy more often completes the valid font-table directory needed to set table_index == 1, which naive's flat budget rarely achieves.
one_line: calibrated energy awards extra havoc to structured font seeds to reach table_index == 1

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score (speed/size/stability) grants compact valid-header font seeds disproportionate havoc iterations, driving them toward the full valid-font structure needed to reach the variable-font cache-create branch.
one_line: calibrated energy amplifies mutations on compact valid-header seeds toward structural GPOS target

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage gives small, fast, near-valid font seeds (with concrete 'kern' table bytes) more havoc mutations per visit, completing the kern-table structure that flips the has_machine_kerning branch false; naive's flat budget dilutes effort across padded seeds.
one_line: calibrated energy completes kern-table structure by concentrating mutations on fast small seeds

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage grants well-behaved KHUDAWADI seeds more havoc energy per visit; the raised per-seed mutation budget raises the probability that havoc replaces the 4-byte follower codepoint with one of the specific inner-switch arm values, which naive's flat budget rarely reaches.
one_line: calibrated energy raises probability of havoc landing the specific 4-byte follower codepoint

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score grants parseable SELECT seeds more havoc mutations; the extra energy raises the probability havoc produces the literal '<' in a valid SELECT expression that compiles to OP_Lt, with no CMP introspection involved.
one_line: calibrated energy raises odds of havoc inserting a literal '<' into a parseable SELECT

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage concentrates mutation budget on faster/smaller/stable SELECT-expression seeds; the extra havoc iterations grow long well-formed expressions that walk the resolver deep enough to set pParse->nErr, triggering WRC_Abort, which naive's flat budget rarely reaches.
one_line: calibrated energy grows long SELECT expressions that accumulate a resolver error

mechanism_summary: CalibrationStage perf_score grants small/fast/stable SELECT seeds more havoc iterations per visit, raising the probability havoc assembles the required literal-arithmetic SELECT body that reaches the codeInteger codegen path.
one_line: calibrated energy amplifies mutations on fast SELECT seeds to reach integer codegen path
